solutions.solution: (d) Let A be the starting point. Final point and Starting point are shown with 10 km distances. At last he reaches D passing B and C. Shortest distance between the points A and D = AD = BC = 15 km
(b) Let Sharada starts from point A and passing through B and C, she reaches D. Clearly, she is in East and at a distance of 15 m from her original position.
(d) Let point A is the starting point and G is the final point of the boy. Now, distance between starting point and final point (AG) = AB - BG = (8 - 3) km = 5 km
(e) AG = BG - AB = (CD + EF) - (AB) = (6 + 8) - 11 = 3 m and GF = 4 m. AF = √(AG)² + (GF)² = √9 + 16 = 5 m
(c) Point G is in the 'North' direction from point A and AG = 3 m (from above solution)
(d) Ram is at a distance of 5 m from his starting point.
(c) DC = EB = 5 m. Required distance = AE = AB + BE = 10 + 5 m = 15 m
